headlines.title: U.S. launches ‘self defense’ strikes on Iran after Apache helicopter shot down
headlines.description: The U.S. launched what it called “self-defense strikes” after an American helicopter was shot down by an Iranian drone over the Strait of Hormuz, as President Trump vowed Iran would “pay the price.” NBC News’ Gabe Gutierrez reports.

headlines.title: Trump says ‘I love the inflation’ as Iran war continues to spike prices
headlines.description: NBC News Correspondents Monica Alba, Courtney Kube and Brian Cheung join Meet the Press NOW to report on recent developments in the war with Iran after the President Trump vowed to attack the country “hard” again while inflation jumps to its highest point since 2023.

headlines.title: Bugs and black mold: What some mobile home park residents see after investors buy in
headlines.description: In recent years, mobile home properties have become less affordable as an influx of large companies have bought up the parks and raised rents and fees.

headlines.title: Graphic: Track U.S., state and county gas prices
headlines.description: Are gas prices declining? NBC News is tracking national, state and county prices at the pump.

headlines.title: Pope Leo blesses world’s tallest church
headlines.description: After 144 years of construction, La Sagrada Familia in Barcelona is nearly complete. NBC News’ Molly Hunter was there as the pope blessed the church.

headlines.title: Bill Gates grilled by lawmakers over Epstein ties
headlines.description: Billionaire and Microsoft co-founder Bill Gates was grilled by lawmakers over his relationship with Jeffrey Epstein. Gates said that Epstein was blackmailing him over his infidelities. NBC News’ Hallie Jackson reports.

headlines.title: Jeffery Lee breathes ‘sigh of relief’ after Alabama’s nitrogen execution is deemed unconstitutional
headlines.description: Moments after a federal judge permanently blocked Jeffery Lee’s execution by nitrogen gas in Alabama, Lee told NBC News he was prepared to keep fighting.

headlines.title: NASA addresses criticism over all-male crew selected for Artemis III test mission
headlines.description: The lack of women sparked “reactions ranging from disappointment to outrage,” Administrator Jared Isaacman said.

headlines.title: Here Are the Top Players to Watch in the 2026 FIFA World Cup
headlines.description: From perennial powerhouses in Europe and South America to underdogs from around the globe, many of soccer’s very best are converging on the United States for the 2026 FIFA World Cup. NBC’s Jesse Kirsch reports for TODAY with the top players to watch.

headlines.title: Referee barred from entering U.S. for World Cup gets a hero’s welcome in Somalia
headlines.description: Somalia isn’t competing at the World Cup, but from the scenes at Mogadishu’s airport, you’d think the country had won the trophy.

headlines.title: Workers at L.A.-area World Cup stadium reach tentative agreement after authorizing strike
headlines.description: Unite Here Local 11, which represents nearly 2,000 cooks, dishwashers, bartenders and concession workers at SoFi Stadium, voted just last week to authorize a strike.

headlines.title: New Jersey police ramp up security as state prepares for World Cup final
headlines.description: As the World Cups heads to 11 cities across the U.S., states are ramping up security to keep fans safe. New Jersey police take NBC News' Sam Brock inside their command center, where they will monitor stadiums, hotels, and training centers ahead of and during matches.
